repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Theorem 1: finite joint-completion LP sharpness for ADEs; current theorem adds IV principal strata and dual exposure channels

published_prior_art.cite: manski_1990_nonparametric_bounds + hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference + schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid: finite bounded-outcome/IV/exposure-bound ingredients, but not this joint finite network-IV projection

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 1 is the parent shared-edge nonsharpness result for ADEs only; no non-parent catalogue entry has shared hidden-network completion across instrument exposure, treatment exposure, and IV compliance

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference and schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid are the closest chain; neither subsumes strict nonrepresentability of stitched dual-map complier bounds under one shared completion

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Theta_C^stitch(P;s,t) is only described prose-wise in Assumption 7; the strict-containment conjecture needs an explicit finite variable/constraint definition of the stitched relaxation outside Exhibit 9.1.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a formal Definition for Theta_C^stitch(P;s,t) with its unitwise hidden-instrument kernels, hidden-treatment kernels, stable-complier kernels, moment-matching constraints, and support-function objective.
Add one sentence after Exhibit 9.1 explaining exactly which strict inequality remains stable under LP value continuity and which primitive coordinates are perturbed to obtain the open class.
Add a Savje location anchor in the Conjecture 1 gap sentence, since the proposal uses Savje as a conceptual comparator for misspecified exposure labels.

recommended_next_step: Patch the stitched-relaxation definition formally, then keep the flagship conjecture and parent-corner reduction unchanged.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal stays in the parent partial-ID network-completion cluster, strictly adds monotone IV take-up plus a second exposure channel, and gives a concrete parent-corner reduction.
